behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff: git-history/age computation + PR-state lookup + staleness rule composition. A single-function unit test can miss wiring bugs (e.g., wrong field passed to age or PR filter). Integration-brake: (a) no explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess in this behavior text, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require "live repo" or real network; that requirement appears explicitly in behavior 4, not here.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local modules with fixture branch-state and PR-state adapters (in-process fakes), then assert stale/non-stale outcomes across threshold and "not in main" combinations.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: The real risk is orchestration at bootstrap: current-branch state -> classifier -> warning renderer/emitter. Unit tests of formatter or classifier alone would miss dropped signal/context fields. Integration-brake: (a) no explicit real subprocess/network requirement, (b) no AI/ML output quality dependency, (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text only says "session-start setup emits" and does not require exercising full OS process/session lifecycle.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke session-start initialization with a stale branch-state fixture and verify one emitted warning includes actionable branch context (branch name/age/next action).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly one local deterministic function mapping inputs to labels. No module boundary, I/O, or nondeterministic dependency is required to catch failures.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for classifier input states to exact label outputs, including edge thresholds and explicit determinism checks.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires "against live repo and PR state," so failures tied to real git state, real PR API contracts, and rendering under real data must be exercised. Mocks can hide contract/time/state drift.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo/branch setup with real PR state (or real API-backed test project) and assert rendered output classification for clean vs stale-no-pr.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is rule integration between branch-state and PR-status interpretation (open/merged). Unit-only tests on either side can miss mis-mapped status semantics. Integration-brake: (a) behavior text does not require live network/API, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but unlike behavior 4 this behavior does not specify "live repo and PR state."
behaviors.test_description: Compose staleness evaluator with PR-status provider fixture cases (`open`, `merged`, `none`) and verify only `none` can yield `stale-no-pr` when other stale conditions hold.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash correctness depends on real git graph/history behavior where commit identities rewrite; simplistic mocks often miss these edge semantics. Real repo operations (and often subprocess git behavior) are the practical failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In an actual git repo fixture, create stale branch history, then perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios; re-run detection and assert branch is no longer classified as stale-no-pr when work is effectively integrated.
